fieldValues.value: RFC-017 Change G: Attachment model clarification
ext:repository (attachment model, tombstone reference-only state, subdirectory support); source-document-meta.json, manifest.json (contentPath)

Five normative clarifications: (1) Single-path attachment via `SourceReference` only — no separate `Relation` edge; (2) Attachments are managed only through core operations — a `SourceReference` whose `sourceId` resolves to no index entry is non-conformant; (3) Tombstone reference-only state — a `sourceDocumentIndex` entry MAY outlive its content; an index entry whose content file is absent is valid (NOT a validation error); an implementation MUST NOT reject or refuse to export a repository on that basis; (4) Subdirectory support — `source-documents/` MAY contain subdirectories; `contentPath` MAY contain forward-slash-separated path segments; implementations MUST resolve `contentPath` relative to `sourceDocumentsPath`; (5) Sidecar co-location — the sidecar MUST reside in the same directory as its content file.

The `documentId` pointer is the durable spine; the content file is a prunable leaf. Keeping the index entry after content removal preserves the reference across content pruning, external-store migration, and federated retrieval transitions. Sidecar co-location ensures `source-documents/` subtrees are self-contained and moveable.
